title: Bulbophyllum spathulatum
taxonomy: Bulbophyllum spathulatum is classified under the family Orchidaceae and is a species of orchid. This genus, Bulbophyllum, is a large and diverse group of orchids with over 2,000 species. Bulbophyllum spathulatum specifically belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae, which contains many of the world's most diverse orchid species. The genus Bulbophyllum is further divided into several subgenera and sections, each with unique characteristics and growth habits. Through its classification, Bulbophyllum spathulatum is recognized as a distinct species within the Orchidaceae family.

intro: Bulbophyllum spathulatum, also known as the Spatulate Bulbophyllum, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Asia and the Pacific Islands. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Bulbophyllum spathulatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
